table_summary: Detailed records of foreign exchange (FX) deals, including transaction parties, amounts, currencies, rates, deal types, statuses, and related financial and operational metadata for each FX transaction.

column_descriptions.description: Text (VARCHAR(20)). Unique identifier for each FX deal. Used to track and reference individual transactions.
Numeric (NUMBER). Indicates if the deal is a swap transaction (1 for swap, 0 for non-swap).
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Unique code identifying the customer involved in the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Account code associated with the customer for this deal. Used for posting and reconciliation.
Text (VARCHAR(401)). Full name of the customer involved in the FX deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Code representing the FX product type (e.g., spot, forward, swap).
Text (VARCHAR(200)). Descriptive name of the FX product.
Text (VARCHAR(200)). Name of the control center or branch handling the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(800)). Additional descriptive information or notes about the deal.
Numeric (NUMBER). Unique identifier for the dealer (employee) who executed the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(100)). Name of the dealer (employee) who executed the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Type/category of the FX product (e.g., spot, forward, swap).
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Sub-category or further classification of the FX product.
Numeric (NUMBER). Identifier for the current workflow or process step of the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(100)). Name of the current workflow or process step.
Numeric (NUMBER). Identifier for the transaction counterparty or related entity.
Text (VARCHAR(200)). Name of the office or branch where the deal was processed.
Date (DATE). The date when the FX deal was initiated or agreed upon.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Indicates if the deal is a buy ('B') or sell ('S') transaction.
Text (VARCHAR(200)). Descriptive label for the buy/sell indicator.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Status code of the account involved in the deal (e.g., active, closed).
Text (VARCHAR(2)). Status code of the deal (e.g., pending, completed, cancelled).
Numeric (NUMBER). Amount being bought in the deal. The currency of this amount is specified by the 'buy_cur_code' column.
Text (CHAR(3)). ISO currency code for the currency being bought (e.g., 'USD', 'MNT', 'EUR').
Numeric (NUMBER). Exchange rate applied to the buy side of the transaction.
Numeric (NUMBER). Amount being sold in the deal. The currency of this amount is specified by the 'sell_cur_code' column.
Text (CHAR(3)). ISO currency code for the currency being sold (e.g., 'USD', 'MNT', 'EUR').
Numeric (NUMBER). Exchange rate applied to the sell side of the transaction.
Numeric (NUMBER). The agreed exchange rate for the overall deal.
Date (DATE). Settlement or value date for the buy side of the transaction.
Date (DATE). Settlement or value date for the sell side of the transaction.
Numeric (NUMBER). Calculated profit or loss from the deal, typically in the base or reporting currency.
Numeric (NUMBER). Market exchange rate at the time of the deal for reference or comparison.
Numeric (NUMBER). Margin amount or percentage applied to the deal, typically for risk or profitability analysis.
Numeric (NUMBER). Spot exchange rate relevant to the deal, if applicable.
Numeric (NUMBER). Interest amount given as part of a swap deal, if applicable.
Numeric (NUMBER). Interest amount received as part of a swap deal, if applicable.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Combination or grouping code for related deals or transactions.
Text (VARCHAR(200)). Descriptive name for the combination or grouping code.
Numeric (NUMBER). Points or basis points adjustment applied to the deal rate, often used in forward or swap deals.
Numeric (NUMBER). Forward exchange rate agreed for the deal, if applicable.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Indicates which side of the deal (buy or sell) is more profitable.
Numeric (NUMBER). Indicates if split value dates are allowed for the deal (1 for allowed, 0 for not allowed).
Text (VARCHAR(3)). First currency in the FX pair (e.g., 'USD', 'MNT').
Text (VARCHAR(3)). Second currency in the FX pair.
Numeric (NUMBER). Indicates if the profitable side can be changed (1 for yes, 0 for no).
Numeric (NUMBER). Calculation method code used for the deal's financial computations.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Indicates if partial payment is allowed for the deal (e.g., '1' for allowed, '0' for not allowed).
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Indicates if off-balance sheet transactions are allowed for the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Code identifying the swap deal, if applicable.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Indicates if the revaluation account can be changed.
Text (VARCHAR(2)). Code for the revaluation method applied to the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Frequency code for how often revaluation occurs.
Numeric (NUMBER). Unit or interval for revaluation frequency.
Numeric (NUMBER). Day when revaluation starts for the deal.
Numeric (NUMBER). Activity code or identifier for the deal's operational process.
Numeric (NUMBER). Number of times the deal can be automatically cancelled or has been cancelled.
Numeric (NUMBER). Interest amount or rate for the first currency in a forward deal.
Numeric (NUMBER). Interest amount or rate for the second currency in a forward deal.
Numeric (NUMBER). Number of decimal places used for currency amounts or rates in the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Code indicating the daily basis convention for interest or rate calculations.
Numeric (NUMBER). Indicates if netting is applied to the deal (1 for yes, 0 for no).
Text (VARCHAR(20)). FX-specific daily basis code for rate or interest calculations.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Indicates if principal amount value date is allowed (1 for yes, 0 for no).
Date (DATE). The most recent date when revaluation was performed for the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(1)). Code indicating the type of netting applied to the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Reference to the netting deal code, if applicable.
Numeric (NUMBER). Code indicating the type of transaction counterparty.
Numeric (NUMBER). Leg number for multi-leg deals (e.g., swaps or complex forwards).
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Daily basis code for the buy side of the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Daily basis code for the sell side of the deal.
Numeric (NUMBER). Transaction amount, possibly used for reporting or reconciliation. The currency context may depend on the deal.
Text (VARCHAR(20)). Account code used for the swap leg of the deal, if applicable.
